title: Comparative Evaluation of Wearable Oxygen-Uptake Estimators Using Persistence, Transition, and Equivalence Analyses: analysis code and result artefacts
description: All analysis code reproducing the paper: preprocessing, per-fold linear training, temporal and tree hyperparameter tuning (Optuna under a 16-fold leave-one-participant-out RMSE objective), statistical analyses (significance, equivalence/power, transition, Bland-Altman agreement, mutual information, predictability horizon), and figure/table generation, together with the shipped prediction and result artefacts. The raw dataset is the publicly available dataset of Beltrame, Amelard, Wong & Hughson (2017), Scientific Reports 7:45738 (CC BY 4.0).
